_doc: Machine-readable release baselines for M3 BRR encoder quality milestone. M3.3 ships phase rotation (SPEC 10.7): encoder-input rotation per atom, lex objective (loop_click_abs, peak_abs_error, rms_error, rotation_offset), block-aligned candidate offsets. All 11 atom fixtures saw loop_click_abs drop to <= pre-M3 values (improvement-gate satisfied). Atom PCM SHAs stay identity-gated per the SPEC 16.9 amendment; BRR / decoded-BRR / post-rotation metric values land here under documentary_snapshot and will shift again at later encoder-changing sub-passes. Three classifications: identity_gated (any drift = regression), behavior_gated (numeric thresholds + improvement invariants), documentary_snapshot (informational; expected to shift on declared milestones).

identity_gated.name: M2_ATOM_128_SINE_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated.locked_at: M2.2 (reclassified to identity_gated at M3.1 per SPEC Â§16.9 amendment)
identity_gated._note: Atom PCM render formula locked at M2.0 SPEC Â§16.9; stability rule promoted to a hard contract at M3.0. M3 encoder work (phase rotation Â§10.7, predictor Â§10.8, pre-emphasis Â§10.9) MUST NOT shift this value.

identity_gated.name: M2_ATOM_64_SINE_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated.locked_at: M2.2 (reclassified to identity_gated at M3.1 per SPEC Â§16.9 amendment)
identity_gated._note: Same stability rule as the 128-sample atom.

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_AMPLITUDE_ZERO_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Atom with amplitude=0 renders all-zero PCM (SHA of 256 zero bytes). Load-bearing for the M3.3 phase-rotation tie-breaker â€” all candidates tie at loop_click_abs=0, lex objective falls through to peak_abs_error/rms_error/rotation_offset which must default to no-rotation.

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_ALL_PARTIALS_ZERO_NORMALIZE_TRUE_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Exercises the normalize max==0 special case in render_to_pcm (max == 0 â†’ skip divide, leaves x all-zero). Produces identical all-zero PCM as M3_ATOM_AMPLITUDE_ZERO; same SHA. No divide-by-zero; no NaN.

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_TWO_PARTIALS_CANCEL_PARTIALLY_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Two equal-amplitude partials with phase_cycles 0.0 and 0.5 cancel mathematically (sin(Î¸) + sin(Î¸+Ï€) = 0) but FP sin is not exact â€” sum is a ULP-scale noise floor. normalize then divides by tiny max, amplifying noise to Â±1.0; * amplitude * 32767 produces non-trivial PCM. The PCM is deterministic and bounded; no NaN. PM may revisit whether normalize should treat near-zero max as zero at M3+ (SPEC Â§16.9 amendment territory).

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_MAX_AMPLITUDE_NO_NORMALIZE_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: 4 partials at harmonics 1..=4, all amp=1.0, normalize=false, atom.amplitude=1.0. Raw sum exceeds 1.0 in places; PCM clamps to Â±32767. Tests the round-half-away-from-zero scaler's defensive clamp.

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_NORMALIZE_FALSE_MULTI_PARTIAL_CLAMP_SAFETY_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Most aggressive overflow path: 8 partials at harmonics 1..=8, all amp=1.0, normalize=false. f64 accumulator has plenty of headroom; verifies anyway.

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_HARMONIC_16_CYCLE_64_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Single partial at harmonic=16, cycle_len_samples=64. Near-Nyquist content (16/64 = quarter sample rate). Critical for M3.4 predictor optimization and M3.6 pre-emphasis â€” high-frequency content is where BRR predictor degenerates and pre-emphasis presets diverge.

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_ALL_8_PARTIALS_MAX_AMP_HARMONICS_1_TO_8_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Full partial-bank stress: 8 partials at harmonics 1..=8, amp=1.0, normalize=true, atom.amplitude=1.0. Bright high-harmonic content.

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_PHASE_CYCLES_0_9999_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Single partial, phase_cycles=0.9999. Phase wraparound boundary. Produces a near-phase=0 waveform; loop_click_abs is 1196 (vs 1197 for the canonical phase=0 sine_128 atom) â€” off by 1 due to the tiny shift.

identity_gated.name: M3_ATOM_CYCLE_256_CANONICAL_SINE_PCM_SHA256
identity_gated._note: Canonical sine at cycle_len_samples=256 â€” parity with the existing 64/128 canonical baselines. loop_click_abs is 606 (smaller than 128's 1197 because larger cycle means smaller last-sample magnitude).

behavior_gated.name: M3_LOOP_CLICK_METRIC_GATING
behavior_gated.rule: M3 sub-passes gate on loop_click_abs only (SPEC Â§10.6); loop_window_rms_delta is diagnostic-only (reports-only) and may promote to a gate at M4+ after the metric has stabilized

behavior_gated.name: M3_PHASE_ROTATION_OBJECTIVE
behavior_gated.rule: phase rotation candidate selection uses lexicographic ordering per SPEC Â§10.7, not a weighted score; smaller rotation_offset wins on full ties (defaulting to no-rotation)

behavior_gated.name: M3_PHASE_ROTATION_CANDIDATE_SET
behavior_gated.rule: candidate offsets are multiples of 16 only: 0, 16, 32, ..., cycle_len_samples - 16 (4 candidates for cycle 64, 8 for cycle 128, 16 for cycle 256); non-block-aligned reserved for M4+ per SPEC Â§10.7

behavior_gated.name: M3_PHASE_ROTATION_LOOP_CLICK_IMPROVEMENT_GATE
behavior_gated.rule: For every atom fixture, M3_ATOM_<NAME>_LOOP_CLICK_ABS_PHASE_ROTATION MUST be <= M3_ATOM_<NAME>_LOOP_CLICK_ABS_PRE_M3. Equal is acceptable (rotation chose offset=0); strictly greater is a regression. Enforced by core/tests/atom_edge_cases.rs::phase_rotation_loop_click_never_regresses_against_pre_m3 (iterates all 11 fixtures).

documentary_snapshot.name: M3_ATOM_128_SINE_LOOP_CLICK_ABS_PRE_M3
documentary_snapshot._note: Pre-M3 loop_click_abs (SPEC Â§10.6) for canonical sine_128 atom under the M2.2 encoder. M3.3 phase rotation MUST produce a value <= this (lexicographic objective per SPEC Â§10.7). Matches the M2.2 M2_ATOM_128_SINE_LOOP_CLICK_SCORE = 1197 value exactly â€” same formula, M3.1 just promotes it from encoder-internal to a formal SPEC-defined metric on the decoded BRR PCM.

documentary_snapshot.name: M3_ATOM_64_SINE_LOOP_CLICK_ABS_PRE_M3
documentary_snapshot._note: Pre-M3 loop_click_abs for canonical sine_64 atom. M3.3 phase rotation MUST produce a value <= this. Matches M2_ATOM_64_SINE_LOOP_CLICK_SCORE = 2407 exactly.

documentary_snapshot.name: M3_ATOM_128_SINE_LOOP_WINDOW_RMS_DELTA_PRE_M3
documentary_snapshot._note: Diagnostic windowed metric (SPEC Â§10.6, window=8) for canonical sine_128 atom under M2.2 encoder. Reports-only at M3; informational baseline.

documentary_snapshot._note: Same; reports-only.

documentary_snapshot._note: SHA-256 of decoded-from-BRR PCM (little-endian i16 bytes) for sine_128 atom under M2.2 encoder. M3.3 phase rotation will shift this. Documentary snapshot â€” expected to shift.

documentary_snapshot._note: Equals the all-zero PCM SHA â€” BRR encode of all-zero PCM round-trips to all-zero decoded PCM.

documentary_snapshot.name: M3_ATOM_AMPLITUDE_ZERO_LOOP_CLICK_ABS_PRE_M3
documentary_snapshot._note: All-zero PCM has no seam. M3.3 phase rotation MUST NOT make this worse (lexicographic objective per Â§10.7); all rotation candidates tie and the smaller-offset tie-breaker selects rotation_offset=0 by default.

documentary_snapshot.name: M3_ATOM_AMPLITUDE_ZERO_LOOP_WINDOW_RMS_DELTA_PRE_M3
documentary_snapshot._note: Diagnostic-only; perfect-seam value.

documentary_snapshot._note: Identical BRR to amplitude_zero â€” both produce all-zero PCM.
